story.iWant: math operations questions that test my calculation abilities
story.soThat: the app can identify if I struggle with addition, subtraction, or multiplication
story.tasks: - Implement BasicOperations component for questions 8-9 (addition/subtraction problems) - Implement WordProblem component for question 10 (dyscalculia-friendly word problems) - Create reusable NumberKeypad component for numeric input (0-9, backspace, submit) - Add question generation logic with randomized numbers - Implement input validation (non-negative integers, max 4 digits) - Record user answers, correctness, and response times - Ensure 24px minimum font size for readability - Add WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance (labels, error announcements)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Basic Operations (Q8-Q9):** - Shows simple arithmetic problem: "12 + 7 = ?" - Answer input: Number keypad (0-9, backspace, submit) - Operations: Mix of addition and subtraction (single-digit and double-digit) - Records: userAnswer, correctAnswer, isCorrect, timeToAnswer
**Word Problem (Q10):** - Shows dyscalculia-friendly word problem (simple context, clear numbers) - Example: "You have 8 apples. You give away 3. How many do you have now?" - Answer input: Number keypad - Records: userAnswer, correctAnswer, isCorrect, timeToAnswer
**Additional Requirements:** - Number keypad rendered using setupCompactAndNumpad pattern from existing codebase - Input validation: Non-negative integers only, max 4 digits - Visual: Large, clear numbers (24px font minimum) for readability - Questions randomized: Numbers vary per session (avoid memorization)
**Prerequisites:** Story 2.3 (Spatial awareness questions implemented)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Assessment & Onboarding Flow
doc.snippet: Epic 2 delivers the initial dyscalculia assessment experience that identifies user weaknesses in number sense, spatial awareness, and math operations. This 10-question wizard serves as the entry point for new users, establishing their personalized training plan weights.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Assessment & Onboarding Flow
doc.snippet: BasicOperations module handles Operations Q1-2 (Mental math) with problem config inputs, producing user answer and correctness. EstimationProblems handles Operations Q3-4 (Approximate answers) with accuracy percentage output.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Assessment & Onboarding Flow
doc.snippet: QuestionResult interface captures: questionId, domain, questionType, correct (boolean), responseTime (ms), userAnswer, expectedAnswer, difficulty (1-3), confidenceBefore (optional 1-5 scale).

doc.title: Discalculas - Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Assessment components located in features/assessment/, routes/AssessmentRoute.tsx. Storage in assessments table (Dexie). Uses multi-step wizard with React Hook Form pattern.

doc.title: Discalculas - Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Components: PascalCase.tsx, Hooks: camelCase.ts with use prefix, Tests: Same + .test.tsx, Types: camelCase.types.ts. Database: snake_case plural tables.

doc.title: Discalculas UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Assessment questions display: Progress bar (10 segments), Question card (white, 20px font), Interactive components with 44px+ tap targets. Math operations use number pad input. No "right/wrong" shown during assessment to maintain non-judgmental experience.

doc.title: Discalculas UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Math Operations Widget: Problem display (large, readable font), Number pad input or multiple choice grid, Clear/backspace button, Submit button. States: Default, Input Active, Submitted, Correct (success green border), Incorrect (warning gold border with hint).

doc.title: Discalculas Epics
doc.snippet: Location: src/features/assessment/components/BasicOperations.tsx, WordProblem.tsx. Number keypad: Reusable component in src/shared/components/NumberKeypad.tsx. Question generation: Randomize numbers within difficulty ranges (e.g., 1-20 for addition). Word problems: Template strings with variable substitution.

file.reason: Reference pattern for question component structure: props interface, result interface, timing with performance.now(), QuestionCard wrapper
Reference pattern for interactive question components with answer validation and timing
Reference pattern for question components: useState for answered state, useCallback for handleAnswer, performance.now() timing, QuestionCard footer with buttons
Most recent spatial question component - follow same structure and patterns for consistency
Shared wrapper component for all assessment questions - use as container for BasicOperations and WordProblem
Question generation patterns with seeded randomization for deterministic tests. Add generateBasicOperationsConfig and generateWordProblemConfig following same patterns
Public API for assessment feature - add new component exports here
shadcn/ui Button component - use for Submit and number keypad buttons with variant="outline"
shadcn/ui Card component - referenced by QuestionCard for consistent styling

story-context.constraints:
**Architectural Constraints:** - Feature-based organization: All operations question components must live in src/features/assessment/components/ - Component naming: PascalCase.tsx (BasicOperations.tsx, WordProblem.tsx, NumberKeypad.tsx) - Export all new components from src/features/assessment/index.ts - Follow existing question component patterns (performance.now() timing, QuestionCard wrapper, result interfaces)
**Data Constraints:** - All timestamps as ISO 8601 strings - Response times in milliseconds (number type) - QuestionResult interface must include: questionId, domain ('operations'), questionType, correct, responseTime, userAnswer, expectedAnswer, difficulty
**UI/UX Constraints:** - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required (4.5:1 contrast, keyboard navigation, ARIA labels) - Minimum 44px tap targets for mobile - 24px minimum font size for numbers and problems - No visual feedback during assessment (no red/green indicators until results page) - Respect prefers-reduced-motion for animations
**Testing Constraints:** - 100% test coverage required (enforced by CI) - Unit tests for question generation logic - Component tests for BasicOperations, WordProblem, NumberKeypad with React Testing Library - Integration tests for answer validation and timing - Use seededRandom for deterministic test question generation
**Performance Constraints:** - Question render time: <100ms from navigation to interactive - Touch response: <50ms tap to visual feedback - Animation FPS: 60fps for transitions

interface.name: BasicOperationsProps
WordProblemProps
NumberKeypadProps
generateBasicOperationsConfig
generateWordProblemConfig
QuestionCard

tests.standards:
**Testing Framework:** Vitest 3.2.4 with React Testing Library 16.3.0 for component tests, Playwright 1.56.1 for E2E tests.
**Coverage Requirements:** 100% statement/branch/function/line coverage enforced by CI. All new code requires comprehensive unit + component tests.
**Testing Patterns:** - Unit tests: Pure functions (question generation, validation logic) - Component tests: User interactions (typing, clicking, keyboard navigation), visual rendering, accessibility (ARIA labels, roles) - Integration tests: Question-to-result data flow, timing accuracy - E2E tests: Full assessment journey covered by Story 2.7
**Test Co-location:** Tests live alongside components with .test.tsx extension
**AAA Pattern:** Arrange (setup), Act (user interaction), Assert (expect outcomes)
tests.ideas:
**BasicOperations.test.tsx:** - AC: Basic Operations Q8-Q9 → Test addition problem renders with correct operands - AC: Basic Operations Q8-Q9 → Test subtraction problem renders with correct operands - AC: Records correctAnswer → Test correct answer submission records isCorrect=true - AC: Records correctAnswer → Test incorrect answer submission records isCorrect=false - AC: Records timeToAnswer → Test response time measured accurately with performance.now() - AC: Number keypad → Test number keypad integration (type digits, backspace, submit) - AC: Input validation → Test max 4 digits enforced - AC: Input validation → Test non-negative integers only - AC: Large font (24px) → Test visual rendering with large, clear numbers - AC: Questions randomized → Test seeded generation produces consistent but different problems
**WordProblem.test.tsx:** - AC: Word Problem Q10 → Test word problem text renders with numbers - AC: Example contexts → Test "apples" context problem - AC: Records correctAnswer → Test correct answer to word problem - AC: Records timeToAnswer → Test timing captured for word problem - AC: Number keypad → Test keypad integration for word problem input - AC: Dyscalculia-friendly → Test simple language and clear number presentation
**NumberKeypad.test.tsx:** - AC: Number keypad (0-9, backspace, submit) → Test all digit buttons (0-9) render and work - AC: Number keypad → Test backspace button removes last digit - AC: Number keypad → Test submit button triggers onSubmit callback - AC: Input validation → Test maxDigits prop enforces limit - AC: Disabled state → Test disabled prop disables all buttons - Accessibility → Test keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Space, Escape) - Accessibility → Test ARIA labels on buttons ("Button 0", "Backspace", "Submit") - Accessibility → Test data-testid passed through for testing
**questions.test.ts (extend existing file):** - AC: Randomization → Test generateBasicOperationsConfig with seed produces deterministic results - AC: Randomization → Test generateBasicOperationsConfig without seed varies - AC: Question types → Test addition operations generate correct answers - AC: Question types → Test subtraction operations generate correct answers (no negative results) - AC: Difficulty ranges → Test operands fall within 1-20 range for easy difficulty - AC: Word problems → Test generateWordProblemConfig with different contexts - AC: Word problems → Test template substitution produces valid problem text
**Integration Tests:** - Test BasicOperations → NumberKeypad → answer submission → result callback flow - Test WordProblem → NumberKeypad → answer submission → result callback flow - Test answer recording includes all required QuestionResult fields - Test performance.now() timing is within reasonable range (<5000ms typical)
**Accessibility Tests:** - Test WCAG 2.1 AA compliance with axe-core - Test keyboard-only operation (no mouse needed) - Test screen reader announcements for validation errors - Test focus management (focus on first input on mount) - Test reduced-motion preference respected
